Harpoon of the Depths  
 

Lore Item Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY
Skill: 1H Piercing Atk Delay: 25
DMG: 8 Dmg Bonus: 35
Backstab DMG: 8
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
DEX: +2 HP: +20
WT: 6.5 Size: MEDIUM
Class: WAR PAL RNG SHD BRD ROG SHM BST
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

Lore Item Placeable

Skill: 1H Piercing Atk Delay: 25

Race: ALL

Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

Item Type:1H Piercing
Stackable:No
Merchant Value:65 pp 2 gp 1 sp 1 cp
Tribute:60

Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2021-07-27 04:57:31
Page Updated:Thu Oct 9th, 2008

Expansion: Ruins of Kunark
Average Price: 100pp Pricing Data...
Rarity: Rare
Level to Attain: 35
[Drops | Comments ]

Drops

This item is found on creatures.

Lake of Ill Omen
NPC Name
a bloodgill goblin


Zone(s) Found In:


Zone Name
Lake of Ill Omen


Uploaded November 27th, 2008
Send a correction
Post Comment
Sell sell sell
QuoteReply
# Feb 03 2003 at 4:24 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1594570761161
Guru
142 posts
If you are hunting the Bloodgills and come across one of these, run to the windmill and sell it ASAP. You will get roughly 55pp for it.

Since they are lore, you can then keep/sell the next one. I would say that 1 in 20 bloodgills carries one of these.

Just be sure you don't lose your camp ;-)
▲ Page top
Poonless
QuoteReply
# Aug 26 2002 at 10:25 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591780280579
View User Forum
9 posts
Killed about 30 of these and no poon

QuoteReply
# Jan 20 2003 at 3:13 PM Rating: Decent

20 posts
I killed about 120 or so of these with my mage and only gotten one.

Edited, Mon Jan 20 14:45:05 2003
▲ Page top
Not that rare and not to shabby
QuoteReply
# Jul 27 2002 at 6:55 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592255244649
View User Forum
36 posts
Well, when i turned lvl 18 I journeyed into the Lake. I was there maybe 5 minutes and i got a tell. Hey, what weapon you using? I said bloodclaw of the stilleto. 6dmg 22dly affect Bloodclaw not bad for that lvl. I wasn't twinked at all so i thought it was pretty good. Then he said well come to the mill and i will give you this harpoon. Not to shabby for a lvl 18. Very nice person. I asked him how much for it and he said its free. I get them all the time and usually just sell to the merchants. He said he averaged 1 in ever 10 kills. That doesn't sound rare to me. Well, now i'm lvl 41 so about time to go fight them myself and find out.

Now i use a grimling mining pick 12dmg 27dly and a Greater owlbear beak 7dmg 23dly but +3str and some other stats. Working on getting a Treaweave, then time to get some new weapons.

I play on the povar server.

Vanderath Dragula
lvl 41 Rogue of the black mask

▲ Page top

▲ Page top
Fatigue with this weapon
QuoteReply
# Mar 31 2001 at 9:07 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
I used to use the Bloodstained Fang, and recently switched to the harpoon (and prefer it). One thing that I have noticed is that, when fighting, my fatigue level(the yellow bar)goes down. Any explanation? Is it because it is heavier? Is there anything I can do for this?
▲ Page top
RE: Fatigue with this weapon
QuoteReply
# May 04 2001 at 2:41 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Yes, it weighs 6.5 and has a delay of 25. Your stamina bar will go down based on three things during melee:

your STA rating
your weapon's weight
how much you swing it (delay, haste etc..)

If fatigue runs out you can't jump and other stats start to go down. If your STA is under 100, you will begin to swing your weapons more slowly. Always important for small race melees to keep it over 100 STA for that reason.

This thing isn't too bad, for a *really* nasty fatigue drainer check out the Bezerker's Axe or something like that I think its 22 dly and weights 8.5 or so hehe.

/g Need Zing please!
Cleric begins to cast a spell
Your body zings with energy.
You swing at a mob for 5 seconds.
/g Need Zing please!
Cleric sighs.
▲ Page top
RE: Fatigue with this weapon
QuoteReply
# Apr 02 2001 at 10:58 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
From what I've seen, experianced and heard the only things that effect how fast you fatigue in fights is the weapon wieght and you STA score. I'm a warrior and often use heavy 2 handed weapons and the way I solve the problem is the kindly ask your cleric to toss you and invigor or some other stamina recovery spell when you get down to about 2 yellow bubs. After that you can't do much running or much more fighting before you're toast.
▲ Page top
RE: Fatigue with this weapon
QuoteReply
# Apr 24 2001 at 6:00 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
I also noticed my fatigue bar going down faster then normal when I had this equipped. I wonder if weapon speed is used in the calculation? I had been using a Forrest Giant Hammer to work up 1hb and my bar did not drop so fast even though the hammer was heavier and the fights took longer because of my low skill.
▲ Page top
RE: Fatigue with this weapon
QuoteReply
# May 29 2001 at 8:58 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592757325424
2 posts
I have noticed that this happens with different weapons. As far as I can tell it is the weight to delay ratio that causes it. I think all weapons make your stamina drop, but for most of them you regain it faster than you lose it so it isn't noticiable. I may be way off, but I think that is how it works.
